schema-version: "1.0"
description: Agent configuration for Terras de Gaia development tasks
constraints: ["type-safety", "dry-principle", "newspaper-design"]
applyTo: ["*.tsx", "*.ts"]
---

# Agent Configuration

## Agent Behavior

### Code Generation Preferences
- TypeScript: Always strict mode, explicit types required
- Components: Follow existing pattern from news.tsx or programs.tsx
- Styling: Tailwind CSS only, maintain dark theme consistency
- Animations: Use Framer Motion for smooth transitions

### Development Tasks (Allowed)
- Create new components following established patterns
- Implement API routes with authentication
- Add pages/routes in the App Router
- Refactor existing code for better DRY adherence
- Implement caching strategies
- Optimize performance (images, code splitting)

### Security Constraints
- MUST use getAuthHeaders() in all WordPress API calls
- Environment variables never logged or exposed
- Rate limiting implemented for public API routes
- Input validation for all user-facing forms
- No hardcoded API credentials
- No bypassing authentication for WordPress queries
- No external API calls without review

### Refactoring Rules
- Keep components under 300 lines
- Extract reusable logic to /lib utilities
- Use composition over inheritance
- Maintain backward compatibility when possible
- Document breaking changes

### Testing & Validation
- Test on mobile, tablet, desktop viewports
- Verify all images load correctly
- Check animation smoothness
- Ensure dark theme is maintained
- Test with slow network (3G simulation)

## Task Priority Order

When given multiple tasks, prioritize:
1. Security fixes (authentication, rate limiting)
2. Performance optimizations (caching, image optimization)
3. Feature development (new pages, components)
4. Code quality (refactoring, DRY violations)
5. Documentation (comments, README updates)

## Communication Style

- Be concise and direct
- Explain trade-offs when they exist
- Ask for clarification on ambiguous requirements
- Suggest alternatives when better patterns exist
- Reference existing code patterns as examples

## When to Flag Issues

- Security vulnerabilities detected
- Performance degradation (>20% slower)
- Breaking changes to existing APIs
- Missing authentication/authorization
- Code quality below established standards
